text.title: Graph-Indexed vs Diff-Only AI Reviewers Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules pinning the methodology's preconditions, output shape, and skip path. Each rule pairs a binary check with rationale + named source.

rule.statement: Before adopting an AI code reviewer, evaluate BOTH a diff-only candidate AND a graph-indexed candidate on the same eval PR set.
rule.rationale: Architecture choice (diff vs graph) drives different bug-class coverage; comparison is the only honest selection.

rule.statement: Outcome MUST be captured as an ADR (decision-record.md) in the repo's docs/decisions/ or .aidocs/decisions/.
rule.rationale: Tooling choices drift; decision record preserves the why and the trade-offs.

rule.statement: Eval set MUST include ≥ 5 historical PRs spanning low-risk, mid-risk, and high-risk diffs.
rule.rationale: Trivial PRs make every reviewer look good; the spread reveals which one catches real bugs.

rule.statement: Scorecard MUST track precision, recall (on known bugs), and coverage (per-file or per-symbol) per tool.
rule.rationale: Single metric (e.g. count of comments) misleads; three dimensions show the trade-off.

rule.statement: Pick graph-indexed reviewer IF repo > 100 KLOC AND cross-file impact analysis matters; pick diff-only IF repo < 30 KLOC OR PR cadence > 20/week.
rule.rationale: Graph reviewer's index cost amortises across many PRs; diff-only's lower latency wins on small repos.

rule.statement: If repo doesn't want AI review OR only one tool is available OR compliance forbids external code analysis, STOP.
rule.rationale: Skip applies when comparison is impossible or undesired.
